pattern: Sorting makes the minimum gap appear only between adjacent elements, so one pass after sorting both finds that gap and collects every pair that achieves it.
hints: The closest two numbers in value must be neighbors once the array is sorted.
Sort first, then scan consecutive pairs to learn the smallest difference before collecting anything.
Do two sweeps over the sorted array: the first finds the minimum adjacent difference, the second appends every adjacent pair whose difference equals it, already in ascending order.

edge_inputs.description: consecutive integers share the same minimum gap of one
single closest pair among spread-out values
negatives and positives mixed, several pairs tie at the minimum
large magnitudes, the minimum gap is between the two largest
evenly spaced so every adjacent pair ties

stress.description: many distinct values over a moderate range, forcing many tied minimum pairs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    pool = list(range(-3 * n, 3 * n))
    rng.shuffle(pool)
    return [pool[:n]]
stress.note: distinct integers guaranteed by sampling without replacement
